---
title: "NCD Financing Impact Case Study"
subtitle: "Reframing health systems research as an operational and economic decision case"
page-layout: article
toc: false
execute:
  echo: false
format:
  html:
    code-tools: false
    code-fold: false
    code-copy: false
---

```{=html}
<div style="padding:4.5rem 0 2.25rem;">
  <span class="section-eyebrow">Project D · Business Impact Case Study</span>
  <h1 class="section-h" style="font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.2rem);margin-top:0.45rem;">Financing chronic care as an operations problem.</h1>
  <p class="section-sub" style="margin-bottom:0;">This page reframes the Kisumu NCD financing work in language that a strategy, operations, or investment audience can absorb quickly: bottlenecks, risk, intervention options, and expected service gains.</p>
</div>
```

## Challenge

Seven public facilities were expected to maintain hypertension and diabetes care, but they operated with:

- no dedicated NCD budget line
- delayed procurement and reimbursement cycles
- limited autonomy to redirect funds when chronic medicine demand changed

## Data approach

The case combined facility records, financial process review, and qualitative evidence from staff and managers to identify where service continuity breaks down.

## Scenario modeling

```{=html}
<div class="cv-stat-grid" style="margin-bottom:2rem;">
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">85.7%</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Facilities reporting frequent stockouts</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">100%</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Facilities without direct spending autonomy</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">18%</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Projected continuity gain with faster approvals</span></article>
  <article class="cv-stat-card"><span class="cv-stat-num">3</span><span class="cv-stat-label">Operational levers modeled</span></article>
</div>
```

| Lever | Modeled effect |
|---|---|
| Faster procurement approval | Fewer medicine interruption days |
| Protected chronic care budget line | More predictable medicine availability |
| Local facility spending discretion | Quicker response to small stock gaps |

## Operational recommendation

The most realistic first move is not a full financing overhaul. It is a tighter operating model:

- protect a minimum NCD budget line
- monitor reimbursement lag as a service KPI
- authorize low-cost local purchases when stockout risk passes a threshold

## Files in this project

- [scenario_model.R](./scenario_model.R) for the scenario calculations
- [data/ncd_financing_scenarios.csv](./data/ncd_financing_scenarios.csv) with example assumptions
- [README.md](./README.md) summarizing the case-study framing

[Back to Projects →](../../index.qmd){.btn-solid style="margin-top:2rem;display:inline-block;"}
